step_by_step_thinking: Based on agent1’s concern about managing the high blood pressure first due to its significant elevation, I concur that option B—Wait, monitor & do the treatment—is appropriate. Ensuring stable blood pressure reduces risks during dental procedures and aligns with best practices in patient care by addressing acute conditions prior to non-urgent interventions. This approach also allows time to reassess if further adjustments to antihypertensive therapy might be necessary before initiating oral prophylaxis safely.
